theme: scriptorium monks deciding which wet-ink pages to shelve
title: The Wet-Ink Scriptorium: A Two-Timescale Shelving Script
strategies: trivial: least-recently-used shelving with no proactive drying -- thrashes on the k+2 desk-loop and pays every wet writeback (reproduces the checker baseline)
greedy: Belady farthest-in-future eviction, no proactive drying -- minimizes fetches but keeps shelving wet pages at the full writeback charge

strong: keep Belady's fetch-optimal eviction order but treat the wet/dry asymmetry as a second timescale -- proactively dry every page that will be shelved wet, in its safe window (after its last inking, before it leaves), converting each De writeback into Pc+Ce
headroom above strong: cost-aware eviction that sometimes shelves a clean page sooner to avoid a drying charge entirely, trading a fetch for a writeback
